nodes.task:
Fix three xterm/session perf issues that all touch the terminal init path: mobile column miscalculation (#6, P2), 1.836s post-connect dead time (#13, P1), and typing/render lag from missing WebGL + eager WebLinks regex (#16, P2).

Files you will edit:
- frontend/src/components/Terminal.tsx
- backend/session.js
- frontend/package.json (only to add @xterm/addon-webgl)

DO NOT touch any other file. Do not refactor unrelated code.

## Issue #6 — mobile column miscalculation (P2)
In frontend/src/components/Terminal.tsx, MIN_FONT_SIZE is currently 13 (line ~16). The issue cites a target of `MOBILE_CONTENT_WIDTH = 72` cols at MIN_FONT_SIZE=10 with CHAR_WIDTH_RATIO=0.6 — but only ~59 cols actually fit on a 390px-wide device, so words like `type` wrap as `typ` + `e` and blog slugs split mid-word.

  Fix: replace the hardcoded MOBILE_CONTENT_WIDTH constant (and any computeFontSize logic that targets it) with a width-derived calculation: `const targetCols = Math.floor(usableWidth / (MIN_FONT_SIZE * CHAR_WIDTH_RATIO));` — clamped sensibly (e.g. min 40, max 80). The exact constants in Terminal.tsx today may differ from the issue text; read lines 1–60 of Terminal.tsx and lines around any `fitAddon.fit()` call (~145, ~216) to see the current shape, then keep the smallest patch that ensures cols never exceeds what the viewport can actually display at the chosen font size.

## Issue #13 — TTI dead time (P1)
Backend (backend/session.js):
  - Around line 561: the welcome auto-type uses 60ms/char delay. Reduce to 20ms/char.
  - Around line 597 (per the issue) there is a 200ms delay before the autoTypeCommand gate opens. Find the actual gate-opening setTimeout in current code (issue line numbers may have drifted) and shrink it to 0–50ms or remove it entirely if safe.
  - Verify the existing 6s safety timeout (currently around lines 354 and 524) is preserved — do NOT remove that fallback.

Frontend (frontend/src/components/Terminal.tsx):
  - There are two `setTimeout(() => fitAddon.fit(), N)` calls (~lines 145 and 208/216). Replace both with a single `requestAnimationFrame(() => fitAddon.fit())` so the first resize ships in <16ms instead of 100ms.
  - Be careful: the resize is what triggers the backend's gate to open and emit welcome. Sending it sooner is the whole point — but make sure resize still fires AFTER `xterm.open()` has actually attached the canvas (rAF callback runs after layout, which is sufficient).

Do NOT change auto-type content or the figlet/welcome script — only timing.

## Issue #16 — WebGL renderer + lazy WebLinks (P2)
In frontend/src/components/Terminal.tsx (~line 79–103):
  1. Add @xterm/addon-webgl as a dep in frontend/package.json (run `npm install --save @xterm/addon-webgl` from frontend/ if the worktree allows it; otherwise edit package.json directly and let the postinstall on next deploy resolve). Pin a version compatible with `@xterm/xterm` 5.5.0.
  2. Load WebglAddon FIRST, in a try/catch, with a context-loss handler that disposes and falls back to canvas:
     ```
     try {
       const webgl = new WebglAddon();
       webgl.onContextLoss(() => webgl.dispose());
       xterm.loadAddon(webgl);
     } catch { /* fall back to canvas */ }
     ```
  3. WebLinksAddon: defer attachment until first user interaction. Replace the immediate `xterm.loadAddon(new WebLinksAddon(...))` with a one-shot listener — e.g. attach on the first `selectionchange` or first `mouseover` on the terminal element, then unbind. Keep the existing `handleLinkActivate` callback as the activation handler.

## Validation
- `cd frontend && npm run build` (or whatever the frontend's build command is per package.json) must succeed with no new TS errors.
- `cd backend && node -c session.js` (syntax check) must succeed.
- If the project has a Playwright TTI script (`scripts/tti-playwright.mjs`), do not need to run it — but read it to confirm your changes don't break the assertions it makes about WS handshake / first-output timing.
- Visually trace: with the changes applied, the auto-type gate should open within ~50ms instead of 200ms, and char-by-char welcome typing should complete in ~140ms (7 chars × 20ms) instead of 420ms.

## Commits
One commit per issue, referencing the issue number with `refs #N` (do NOT auto-close):
  - `terminal: derive mobile target cols from actual viewport width (refs #6)`
  - `terminal: collapse init delays from ~600ms to <100ms (refs #13)`
  - `terminal: add WebGL renderer, lazy-load WebLinks addon (refs #16)`

No Co-Authored-By trailer.

flt workflow pass when tests are green and your coder.md handoff is written.

Fix two font-loading issues: font-display: swap corrupts box-drawing glyphs on mobile (#5, P2) and the Nerd Font WOFF2 files are oversized (#14, P2).

Files you will edit:
- frontend/src/app/globals.css
- frontend/public/fonts/jetbrains-mono-nerd.css
- frontend/public/fonts/JetBrainsMonoNerdFontMono-Regular.woff2 (replace with subset)
- frontend/public/fonts/JetBrainsMonoNerdFontMono-Bold.woff2 (replace with subset)

DO NOT delete the existing TTF files — the @font-face declarations list them as a fallback `format('truetype')` source.

## Issue #5 — font-display: block (P2, do this first; trivial and unblocks #14)
In frontend/src/app/globals.css around lines 10 and 19, change `font-display: swap` to `font-display: block` for both Regular and Bold @font-face declarations.

In frontend/public/fonts/jetbrains-mono-nerd.css, change every `font-display: swap` to `font-display: block` (4 occurrences per the issue: lines 10, 18, 28, 36 — verify by reading the file).

Why block: on mobile, `swap` lets the browser paint with Menlo first; xterm measures cell width from Menlo, then never re-measures when the Nerd Font arrives, so block characters render stippled. With `font-display: block`, the browser holds for up to ~3s before rendering — and font caching is already configured (max-age=31536000), so this only impacts uncached first visits.

## Issue #14 — Subset Nerd Font WOFF2 (P2)
The two woff2 files are ~1.0 MB each. Subset them to the Unicode ranges actually used:
  U+0020–U+007F (Basic Latin)
  U+00A0–U+00FF (Latin-1 Supplement)
  U+2500–U+259F (Box Drawing + Block Elements)
  U+25A0–U+25FF (Geometric Shapes)
  U+E000–U+E00A (Pomicons subset used by zsh prompt)
  U+E0A0–U+E0D4 (Powerline arrows + separators)

Approach:
  1. Confirm `pyftsubset` (from fonttools) and `brotli` are available: `python3 -c 'import fontTools, brotli'`. If not, install via `pip install --user fonttools brotli` — if pip isn't available either, signal `flt workflow fail "font subsetting tool unavailable in coder env"` and stop AFTER #5 has been committed (so #5 still ships).
  2. Run, from the frontend/public/fonts/ directory:
     ```
     pyftsubset JetBrainsMonoNerdFontMono-Regular.woff2 \
       --unicodes='U+0020-007F,U+00A0-00FF,U+2500-259F,U+25A0-25FF,U+E000-E00A,U+E0A0-E0D4' \
       --output-file=JetBrainsMonoNerdFontMono-Regular.subset.woff2 \
       --flavor=woff2 --layout-features='*' --no-hinting
     ```
     and the same for Bold.
  3. Verify the new files render the needed glyphs (rough sanity: file size should land in 100–250 KB range; if it's <30KB, your unicode list is wrong).
  4. Replace the originals: `mv JetBrainsMonoNerdFontMono-Regular.subset.woff2 JetBrainsMonoNerdFontMono-Regular.woff2` (and Bold). Keep the TTFs as fallback.

Before committing the subset, grep all terminal scripts for codepoints outside the subset:
  `grep -rPn '[^\x00-\x7f]' container/scripts/*.sh | head -50`
If you spot any U+25xx, U+E0xx, or other range outside the subset list, EXPAND the unicode list to cover them and re-subset. Do NOT ship a subset that drops glyphs the welcome banner or zsh prompt actually emits.

## Validation
- After #5: visual check that globals.css and jetbrains-mono-nerd.css show `font-display: block` everywhere.
- After #14: `ls -lh frontend/public/fonts/JetBrainsMonoNerdFontMono-*.woff2` should each be <300KB; combined ≤ ~500KB (down from ~2.1MB).
- `cd frontend && npm run build` succeeds.

## Commits
One commit per issue, referencing the issue number with `refs #N`:
  - `fonts: switch font-display from swap to block (refs #5)`
  - `fonts: subset Nerd Font WOFF2 to ~200KB per face (refs #14)`

If #14 cannot be completed due to missing tooling, commit only #5 and signal `flt workflow fail` with the reason — but only AFTER #5 is committed.

No Co-Authored-By trailer.

Fix four Next.js routing/SEO issues: soft 404s (#7, P2), generic <title> on every terminal page (#8, P2), missing OG/Twitter meta (#11, P3), and nav bar missing about/contact (#12, P3).

Files you will edit / create:
- frontend/src/app/layout.tsx (modify root metadata)
- frontend/src/app/page.tsx (add per-route metadata for the home `/` route — small)
- frontend/src/app/[...slug]/page.tsx (currently a 7-line re-export — needs real generateMetadata + notFound logic)
- frontend/src/app/not-found.tsx (CREATE — renders the same terminal UI but returns 404 status; or rely on notFound() throw from the slug route)
- frontend/src/components/SiteHeader.tsx (add about + contact links)

DO NOT touch Terminal.tsx or session.js — those belong to a different node and changing them here will create a merge conflict.

DO NOT touch the existing per-blog metadata in frontend/src/app/blog/[slug]/ — blog posts already have correct titles per the issue. Use that file's pattern as a reference.

## Known recognised terminal commands
Read container/scripts/*.sh to enumerate the supported root-level commands. As of writing they are: home (root /), about, contact, projects, resume, blog, agentelo, harness, hone, flt, dotfiles, term-site, trade-up-bot, skyblock-qol, stm32-games. Verify by listing container/scripts/. Anything not in this list (and not a /blog/<slug> path that resolves in frontend/src/app/blog/[slug]) is a 404.

## Issue #7 — proper 404 status (P2)
In frontend/src/app/[...slug]/page.tsx, replace the bare re-export with a real component that:
  1. Reads the slug param.
  2. Computes the first slug segment.
  3. If the first segment is not in the recognised-commands list above (and not `t` — note that `/t/<command>` is a valid alias prefix per existing routes, see the `t/resume`/`t/blog` Links in SiteHeader/BlogUnifiedPage), call `notFound()` from `next/navigation`.
  4. Otherwise, render the home page component (preserve current behaviour).

Create frontend/src/app/not-found.tsx that renders the same SiteHeader + Terminal component (so the terminal still loads and the user can type a recovery command), but Next.js will automatically attach a 404 status to it. The terminal can either auto-type `home` or do nothing — your call. Aim for minimal new code; reuse the home page's layout.

Verify: `curl -sI http://localhost:3000/totally-fake-route` returns `HTTP/1.1 404`.

## Issue #8 — per-route <title> (P2)
In frontend/src/app/[...slug]/page.tsx export a `generateMetadata({ params })` function that returns a per-route title using the route's first segment. Suggested mapping:
  - about    → `About — Tim Waldin`
  - contact  → `Contact — Tim Waldin`
  - projects → `Projects — Tim Waldin`
  - resume   → `Resume — Tim Waldin`
  - blog     → `Blog — Tim Waldin`
  - agentelo → `agentelo — Tim Waldin`
  - (etc. for the rest of the recognised commands)
  - default fallback for unknown command → `Tim Waldin` (the not-found page handles the actual 404)

In frontend/src/app/page.tsx, override the root `/` title to `Tim Waldin — Portfolio` via an exported `metadata` constant (DON'T change the home route's existing imports/render).

In frontend/src/app/layout.tsx, change the existing static `title: 'twaldin portfolio'` to use Next.js's title template pattern so per-page titles compose properly:
  ```
  title: { default: 'Tim Waldin — Portfolio', template: '%s' }
  ```
  (using `%s` keeps the per-route titles you set above as the full title; switch to `'%s | Tim Waldin'` if you prefer suffixing, but match the style the per-route titles already include the name.)

## Issue #11 — Open Graph + Twitter Card (P3)
In frontend/src/app/layout.tsx's `metadata` export, add `openGraph` and `twitter` blocks. Required minimum:
  ```
  openGraph: {
    title: 'Tim Waldin — Portfolio',
    description: 'Interactive terminal portfolio',
    url: 'https://tim.waldin.net',
    siteName: 'tim.waldin.net',
    type: 'website',
  },
  twitter: {
    card: 'summary_large_image',
    title: 'Tim Waldin — Portfolio',
    description: 'Interactive terminal portfolio',
  },
  metadataBase: new URL('https://tim.waldin.net'),
  ```

If there's an existing site OG image (check frontend/public/ for any *.png you might use — desktop-blog.png exists at repo root and is plausibly a candidate, but only reference an image that's already served at a stable public URL; otherwise omit `images` and let the card render text-only).

In the slug route's generateMetadata, return a per-route `openGraph.title` and `openGraph.url` so each terminal route shares correctly.

## Issue #12 — nav bar (P3)
In frontend/src/components/SiteHeader.tsx around line 53–59, add two new <Link> entries between resume and the closing </nav>:
  ```
  <Link href="/t/about" onClick={hardNav('/t/about')} style={linkStyle}>about</Link>
  <Link href="/t/contact" onClick={hardNav('/t/contact')} style={linkStyle}>contact</Link>
  ```
Keep the same separator style as the other items.

## Validation
- `cd frontend && npm run build` succeeds with no new TS errors.
- View-source on `/` shows the OG meta tags.
- View-source on `/t/about` shows `<title>About — Tim Waldin</title>`.
- A request to `/totally-fake-route` returns HTTP 404 (test in dev with curl after `npm run dev`).
- Nav bar visually shows: home · blog · projects · resume · about · contact.

## Commits
One commit per issue, referencing the issue number with `refs #N`:
  - `app: return real 404 for unknown terminal routes (refs #7)`
  - `app: per-route <title> for terminal pages (refs #8)`
  - `app: add OpenGraph and Twitter Card meta (refs #11)`
  - `header: add about and contact to nav (refs #12)`

No Co-Authored-By trailer.

Fix two small content/UX issues: stale /about bio (#9, P2) and resume page showing wrong CWD (#10, P2).

Files you will edit:
- container/scripts/about.sh
- container/scripts/resume.sh OR container/Dockerfile (whichever fixes the CWD — see analysis below)

DO NOT touch any other script. Do not refactor shared-functions.sh.

## Issue #9 — stale about bio (P2)
In container/scripts/about.sh the `create_box "About Me"` block currently reads:
  ```
  Hi, I'm Tim. I'm 19 and from San Francisco, CA, and
  Currently, I'm a Web Programming and Design student at Purdue University
  and am working on tradeupbot.app (check it out!)

  In my spare time I like to make side projects, like the one you're reading.

  type contact to see my contact info
  type projects to see my projects
  ```

Replace the bio with current accurate copy. Use this as the canonical replacement (the user can tweak later — the priority is getting the obviously stale claims off the live site):
  ```
  Hi, I'm Tim. I'm based in San Francisco, CA, and I work on AI agent tooling.
  Recent projects include agentelo (a multi-model agent leaderboard), hone
  (prompt-honing harness), and harness/harness-bench (agent benchmarking).

  In my spare time I tinker with side projects like the one you're reading.

  type projects to see what I'm working on
  type contact to get in touch
  ```
Keep the existing `create_box`, `$PURPLE`, and width=80 args unchanged — only swap the body text. Preserve the `\n` formatting style the create_box helper expects.

## Issue #10 — resume CWD (P2)
The issue: navigating to /t/resume shows the prompt as `tim.waldin.net ~/projects` instead of `tim.waldin.net ~`. Root cause investigation:
  - container/Dockerfile aliases like `alias projects='cd ~/projects && /home/portfolio/scripts/projects.sh'` change the shell's CWD to ~/projects when the user runs `projects`.
  - There is currently NO alias for `resume` (search container/Dockerfile). So when the auto-type sends `resume` after a previous `projects` invocation (or after pool warm-up that visits projects), the shell stays in ~/projects and the prompt reflects that.
  - The other plausible cause: the welcome.sh or warm-pool sequence cd's into ~/projects. Verify by reading container/scripts/welcome.sh and container/scripts/auto-welcome.sh.

Fix: add an alias for `resume` in container/Dockerfile that cd's to ~ first, mirroring the existing alias pattern. Add to BOTH the .zshrc block (~line 187) and the .bashrc block (~line 281):
  ```
  alias resume='cd ~ && /home/portfolio/scripts/resume.sh'
  ```
While you're there, audit the alias list against the recognised commands (about, contact) — if those are also missing aliases and any of them currently shows wrong CWD, add the same `cd ~ && ...` alias for them. (about + contact may not have shown the bug because nobody types them after `projects` — but adding them defensively is safe and zero-risk.)

Alternative if Dockerfile aliases feel wrong: prepend `cd ~` as the first non-shebang line in container/scripts/resume.sh. The Dockerfile alias is preferred because it's consistent with how every other content command in the alias block already works.

## Validation
- Local: `bash -c 'source <(grep "^echo .alias" container/Dockerfile | sed "s/echo .//;s/. >>.*//")' ` is overkill — instead, eyeball the Dockerfile diff to confirm the new alias line matches the format of the others (single quotes, &&, full path).
- A docker build is NOT required for this node to pass; the deploy step will rebuild. Just confirm the Dockerfile is syntactically intact (no unbalanced quotes) by running `docker build --check -f container/Dockerfile container/` if Docker is available, otherwise visual inspection.

## Commits
One commit per issue, referencing the issue number with `refs #N`:
  - `about: refresh bio with current role and projects (refs #9)`
  - `container: alias resume to cd ~ first to fix prompt CWD (refs #10)`

No Co-Authored-By trailer.
